Container.Name: Hoodik
Container.Overview: Hoodik is a lightweight, self-hosted, end-to-end encrypted cloud storage server. All encryption and decryption happens in the browser — the server never sees your plaintext data. Built with Rust and Vue 3 for speed and security. Supports file uploading, downloading, sharing via encrypted links, and S3-compatible object storage backends (AWS S3, MinIO, Backblaze B2, etc.).

Config.Name: DATA_DIR
Config.Description: Maps a location for the data directory of the drive where all the data will be stored.

Config.Name: PORT
Config.Description: Application port

Config.Name: MAILER_TYPE
Config.Description: Email sender, currently supported: smtp

Config.Name: SMTP_ADDRESS
Config.Description: Address of the SMTP server (if MAILER_TYPE=smtp)

Config.Name: SMTP_USERNAME
Config.Description: Username of the SMTP server (if MAILER_TYPE=smtp)

Config.Name: SMTP_PASSWORD
Config.Description:
Password of the SMTP server (if MAILER_TYPE=smtp)
For Gmail, you will have to use https://myaccount.google.com/u/0/apppasswords

Config.Name: SMTP_PORT
Config.Description:
Port of the SMTP server (if MAILER_TYPE=smtp)
Not required, default is automatically set to 465

Config.Name: SMTP_TLS_MODE
Config.Description: SMTP TLS mode (if MAILER_TYPE=smtp). Auto-detected from port if not set. Values: implicit (port 465), starttls (port 587), none.

Config.Name: SMTP_DEFAULT_FROM_NAME
Config.Description: Default FROM display name (if MAILER_TYPE=smtp). Example: HoodikDrive - Unraid

Config.Name: SMTP_DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL
Config.Description: Default FROM email address (if MAILER_TYPE=smtp). Example: user@example.com

Config.Name: APP_URL
Config.Description: This is the application path as seen from the browser, you should use the URL you will be entering to get to the application, eg. https://hoodik.local

Config.Name: APP_CLIENT_URL
Config.Description: Application client URL, this will in 99.9% of the cases be exactly the same as the APP_URL, but in case you are hosting a separate frontend for this application you can define it to its own address.

Config.Name: APP_NAME
Config.Description: Application display name, shown in the browser tab and emails.

Config.Name: SSL_CERT_FILE
Config.Description: Location of the SSL cert file for the application. If the file doesn't exist, it will be created and filled with a self signed cert. That will only happen if both cert and key files are missing.

Config.Name: SSL_KEY_FILE
Config.Description: Location of the SSL key file for the application. If the file doesn't exist, it will be created and filled with a self signed key. That will only happen if both cert and key files are missing.

Config.Name: STORAGE_PROVIDER
Config.Description: Storage backend for file chunks. Use 'local' for filesystem storage (default) or 's3' for S3-compatible object storage.

Config.Name: S3_BUCKET
Config.Description: S3 bucket name (required if STORAGE_PROVIDER=s3).

Config.Name: S3_REGION
Config.Description: S3 region (required if STORAGE_PROVIDER=s3). For MinIO or other self-hosted services, any value works.

Config.Name: S3_ENDPOINT
Config.Description: S3 endpoint URL (required if STORAGE_PROVIDER=s3). Example: https://s3.amazonaws.com or http://minio:9000 for self-hosted.

Config.Name: S3_ACCESS_KEY
Config.Description: S3 access key ID (required if STORAGE_PROVIDER=s3).

Config.Name: S3_SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: S3 secret access key (required if STORAGE_PROVIDER=s3).

Config.Name: S3_PATH_STYLE
Config.Description: Use path-style S3 URLs instead of virtual-hosted-style. Set to 'true' for MinIO and most self-hosted S3 services, 'false' for AWS S3.

Config.Name: S3_PREFIX
Config.Description: Optional key prefix for all S3 objects (e.g. 'hoodik/'). Useful for sharing a bucket with other services.
